name: Alto de Galleiro Loop
description: Hike the Alto de Galleiro — circular trail for panoramic views of the Galician coast, including Vigo and the Cíes Islands.
region_intro: Hiking around Pazos De Borbén offers diverse terrain within the picturesque province of Pontevedra, Spain. The region features river valleys carved by the Borbén, Barragán, Pozo Negro, and Tea rivers, creating fertile areas and dense forests. The landscape combines mountainous sections with abundant greenery, providing varied elevations. Hikers can explore well-established routes through these natural features. Best hiking trails around Pazos De Borbén The most popular hiking route is Río Barragan – Fraga trail (PR-G164), a 10.7 miles (17.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the lush Fragas del Río Barragán. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alto de Galleiro — circular, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This circular route offers varied terrain and takes approximately 2 hours 50 minutes. Local hikers also love the Castillo de Soutomayor to Monte Monzón — circular by the Puentes del río Verdugo, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historical points, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Pazos De Borbén is defined by river valleys, dense forests, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Pazos De Borbén are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,500 reviews. More than 9,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Pazos De Borbén's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Pazos De Borbén?
A: Pazos De Borbén offers a diverse landscape characterized by river valleys, dense forests, and mountainous sections. You'll find varied elevations, with trails carving through areas shaped by rivers like the Borbén, Barragán, Pozo Negro, and Tea. The region is known for its lush greenery and traditional Galician scenery, including quaint stone villages and 'hórreos' (traditional granaries).

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hiking trails in Pazos De Borbén?
A: Yes, Pazos De Borbén has options for various ability levels, including easy strolls. Out of the 99 available routes, 33 are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These trails often follow river paths or explore less strenuous forested areas.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Pazos De Borbén?
A: Hikers can discover a wealth of natural beauty and historical points. The region is rich in river valleys and forests, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991228698/rio-barragan-fraga-trail-pr-g164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fragas del Río Barragán&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter traditional Galician architecture in villages like Moscoso. Nearby attractions include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/921667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza da Moura&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1449456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eiras Dam&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider trails leading to viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4794959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Vigo estuary&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Monte A Peneda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Pazos De Borbén?
A: Yes, Pazos De Borbén offers several circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375170645/alto-de-galleiro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Galleiro — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.8 km path that takes approximately 2 hours 50 minutes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374933484/el-monte-monzon-from-castillo-de-soutomayor-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Monte Monzón from Castillo de Soutomayor — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 11 km long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Pazos De Borbén?
A: Pazos De Borbén is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year due to its mild Galician climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, ideal for exploring the river valleys and forests. Summer can be warm but is still suitable, especially for shaded routes. Winter hikes are also possible, though some trails might be wetter.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Pazos De Borbén?
A: The region's diverse trail network includes many easy routes suitable for families. These often feature gentle gradients and scenic points of interest, such as riverbanks or traditional villages. Look for trails classified as 'easy' to find the most suitable options for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Pazos De Borbén?
A: Many trails in Pazos De Borbén are suitable for dogs, allowing you to enjoy the natural surroundings with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about Pazos De Borbén?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the river valleys and dense forests, and the well-established routes that cater to various skill levels. The blend of natural beauty and traditional Galician elements also receives positive feedback.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by historical sites or cultural points of interest?
A: Yes, several routes in Pazos De Borbén offer glimpses into the region's rich heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374950078/castillo-de-soutomayor-to-monte-monzon-circular-by-the-puentes-del-rio-verdugo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Soutomayor to Monte Monzón — circular by the Puentes del río Verdugo&lt;/a&gt; trail, for example, leads through river valleys and past historical points. You might also encounter traditional 'hórreos' and quaint stone villages, or pass by local churches like San Sadurniño de Amoedo and Santa María de Pazos.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Pazos De Borbén?
A: While Pazos De Borbén is best explored by car for direct access to many trailheads, some local bus services might connect to larger towns or villages near hiking areas. For specific routes, it's advisable to check local transport schedules in advance, as services can be limited, especially to more remote starting points.

Q: Are there designated parking areas for hikers in Pazos De Borbén?
A: Many popular trailheads and points of interest in Pazos De Borbén have informal or designated parking areas. In villages and near attractions, you can often find places to park your vehicle. However, for more remote trails, parking might be limited, so it's good to plan your starting point accordingly.
tips: Monte O Galleiro (748 m), located in the municipality of Mos, near Vigo, is a popular destination for hiking due to its well-marked routes and its impressive panoramic views of the Vigo Estuary and the surrounding area. It is part of the Serra do Galleiro and is surrounded by native vegetation, pine forests and eucalyptus trees. It is ideal for outdoor activities and enjoying Galician nature.
On a small plateau at the top of this mountain, a natural pool accumulates rainwater that falls on this high mountain. Without a doubt, many animals use this beautiful natural lagoon after sunset.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Alto de Galleiro — circular hike?
faqs.answer: The Komoot data suggests this hike takes approximately 2 hours and 48 minutes of moving time. However, with stops to enjoy the panoramic views, explore highlights like the Alto de Galleiro summit, or observe the "infinite lagoon," plan for a total duration of 3 to 4 hours, depending on your pace and how often you pause.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Alto de Galleiro — circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is predominantly dirt paths (about 98%), with a small asphalt section. You'll encounter rocky and sometimes 'bald mountain' landscapes. Expect steep climbs over stones and along firebreaks, especially when ascending to the peaks. There's also a challenging 60-meter segment near the village of Mouro where the path is not clearly defined.

faqs.question: Is the Alto de Galleiro — circular hike suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as 'moderate' by Komoot, but sections are considered 'difficult' due to steep ascents, rocky terrain, and a lack of a clear path in one area. It requires good fitness and sure-footedness. While rewarding, it might be too challenging for beginners or young children. More experienced hikers will find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Alto de Galleiro — circular trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the municipality of Ponteareas. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, it's common for trails in this region to have informal parking near the starting point, often close to villages like Mouro. It's advisable to check local maps or recent hiker reports for the most convenient and safe parking options.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to impressive panoramic vistas throughout the hike. Key viewpoints include the geodetic vertices of Alto de Galleiro and Alto de Salgueirón, offering breathtaking views of the San Simón estuary, Vigo, and the Cíes Islands. A unique feature is the intermittent pond, often called the "infinite lagoon," near the summit, which provides stunning reflections when full. You might also encounter the 'musical' granite formation, Pedra Catanga, and potentially wild horses.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established trails. Approximately 15% of the hike follows the Sendeiro das Greas, and about 6% coincides with the GR 58 Sendeiro das Greas.

faqs.question: Is the Alto de Galleiro — circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The presence of wild horses in the region suggests that dogs are generally allowed. However, due to the challenging terrain and the presence of wildlife, it's highly recommended to keep dogs on a leash for their safety and to respect the natural environment and other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Alto de Galleiro — circular?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking in Galicia, offering milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer can be hot, and some sections are exposed. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for potentially wet, windy, and colder conditions, especially at higher elevations, and ensure you have appropriate gear for slippery or muddy paths.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike in Serra do Galleiro?
faqs.answer: Currently, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails in the Serra do Galleiro, including the Alto de Galleiro — circular route. However, always practice Leave No Trace principles and respect local regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: Are there any local secrets or insider tips for this hike?
faqs.answer: For the most dramatic views, aim to hike on a clear day to fully appreciate the expansive panoramas of the Ría de Vigo and the Cíes Islands. If you visit after a period of rain, you might be lucky enough to see the 'infinite lagoon' near the summit full, creating a spectacular reflective surface. Also, keep an eye out for the Pedra Catanga, a unique granite formation with local legends.

faqs.question: What should I bring for the Alto de Galleiro — circular hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate to difficult rating and varied terrain, sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are essential. Bring plenty of water, as sources on the trail are limited (e.g., a fountain in Mouro). Layers of clothing are advisable due to changing weather conditions, and sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is important for exposed sections. A map or GPS device is also recommended, especially for the less-defined sections.
